name: Great Pyramid of Giza
description: The oldest and largest of the pyramids in the Giza pyramid complex, a true marvel of ancient engineering and the only remaining wonder of the ancient world.
tips: Best visited early morning to avoid crowds
Wear comfortable walking shoes

name: Great Sphinx of Giza
description: A limestone statue of a reclining sphinx, a mythical creature with the body of a lion and the head of a human, guarding the ancient plateau.
tips: Great photo ops near the front paws
Bring sun protection

name: Saqqara Necropolis
description: A vast, ancient burial ground in Egypt, serving as the necropolis for the Ancient Egyptian capital, Memphis, featuring the famous Step Pyramid of Djoser.
tips: Hire an official guide
Less crowded than the main Giza plateau

name: Egyptian Museum
description: Home to an extensive collection of ancient Egyptian antiquities, housing over 120,000 items including the magnificent golden mask of King Tutankhamun.
tips: Photography requires a special ticket
Plan for at least a half-day

name: Khan el-Khalili
description: A famous bazaar and souq in the historic center of Cairo. The bustling alleys are packed with colorful lanterns, spices, jewelry, and traditional crafts.
tips: Bargaining is expected
Enjoy mint tea at El Fishawy cafe

name: Cairo Citadel
description: A medieval Islamic-era fortification in Cairo, built by Salah ad-Din and offering sweeping panoramic views of the entire sprawling city below.
tips: Dress modestly for the mosques
Visit at sunset for incredible views

name: Al-Azhar Mosque
description: One of Cairo's earliest surviving mosques and the site of the world's oldest university, a stunning example of classic Islamic architecture and scholarship.
tips: Shoes must be removed
Women should cover their hair

name: Coptic Cairo
description: A unique neighborhood in Old Cairo that encompasses the Babylon Fortress, the Hanging Church, the Greek Church of St. George, and many other historical sites.
tips: Respectful attire required
Visit the Coptic Museum nearby

name: Al-Azhar Park
description: A lush, green sanctuary in the heart of Islamic Cairo. It offers beautifully manicured gardens, fountains, and excellent views of the surrounding historic minarets.
tips: Great for a family picnic
Lakeside cafes offer excellent local food

name: Karnak Temple
description: A vast mix of decayed temples, chapels, pylons, and other buildings. The Great Hypostyle Hall is a magnificent forest of giant ancient pillars.
tips: Attend the Sound and Light show at night
Go early to beat the intense heat

name: Valley of the Kings
description: The ancient burial ground of many of Egypt's New Kingdom rulers, featuring intricately decorated tombs carved deep into the desert rock.
tips: Tutankhamun's tomb requires an extra ticket
No photography inside tombs without a pass

name: Luxor Temple
description: A large Ancient Egyptian temple complex located on the east bank of the Nile River. It is beautifully illuminated at night, offering a magical experience.
tips: Best visited at dusk or night
Located right in the city center

name: Hatshepsut Temple
description: The striking mortuary temple of Queen Hatshepsut, built into the spectacular limestone cliffs of Deir el-Bahari, standing as a masterpiece of ancient architecture.
tips: Take the small electric train from the entrance
Very little shade, bring a hat

name: Colossi of Memnon
description: Two massive monumental stone statues of Pharaoh Amenhotep III, standing proudly on the west bank of the Nile, remnants of a once-massive mortuary temple.
tips: A quick stop on the way to the Valley of the Kings
Free to view

name: Philae Temple
description: An island-based temple complex dedicated to the goddess Isis, relocated entirely by UNESCO to save it from the rising waters of Lake Nasser.
tips: Accessed via a short motorboat ride
The night show is highly recommended

name: Abu Simbel Temples
description: Two massive rock temples featuring gigantic statues of Ramesses II, built to showcase the might of the pharaoh and perfectly aligned with the sun twice a year.
tips: Usually requires an early morning convoy ride from Aswan
Absolutely unmissable

name: Unfinished Obelisk
description: The largest known ancient obelisk, lying carved out of the bedrock in a stone quarry. It offers incredible insight into ancient Egyptian stone-working techniques.
tips: Very hot in the quarry, go early
Good for understanding ancient engineering

name: Bibliotheca Alexandrina
description: A magnificent modern library and cultural center built to commemorate the ancient Library of Alexandria. It features a stunning tilted disc architectural design.
tips: Check out the antiquities museum inside
English tours are available

name: Qaitbay Citadel
description: A 15th-century defensive fortress located on the Mediterranean coast, built upon the exact ruins of the famous Lighthouse of Alexandria.
tips: Great views of the Mediterranean Sea
Enjoy a walk along the Corniche afterwards

name: Montazah Palace Gardens
description: Expansive royal gardens surrounding the striking Montazah Palace. The lush grounds feature rare plants, peaceful coves, and striking sea views.
tips: Perfect for afternoon strolls
Palace interior is closed to the public

name: Giftun Island
description: A stunning national park island offering pristine white-sand beaches, crystal clear waters, and some of the best shallow-water snorkeling in the Red Sea.
tips: Book a boat tour in advance
Bring your own snorkeling gear if possible

name: Makadi Bay Water World
description: Egypt's largest aqua park, featuring 50 thrilling water slides and pools designed for both adrenaline seekers and families looking for a fun day out.
tips: Arrive right at opening to claim a sunbed
Lockers are available

name: Ras Mohammed National Park
description: A world-renowned diving and snorkeling haven at the southern tip of the Sinai Peninsula, famed for its incredibly vibrant coral reefs and rich marine life.
tips: Don't miss the mangrove channel
Requires a passport for entry

name: Naama Bay
description: The bustling heart of Sharm El Sheikh, offering sandy beaches by day and a vibrant promenade filled with restaurants, cafes, and nightlife by dusk.
tips: Great for beginner divers
Plenty of dining options right on the beach

name: White Desert
description: An otherworldly landscape of surreal, snow-white chalk rock formations sculpted by the wind over centuries, resembling giant mushrooms and animals.
tips: Overnight camping under the stars is a must
Nights can get very cold

name: Crystal Mountain
description: A magnificent ridge composed entirely of sparkling quartz crystals and barite, glistening brightly under the intense Egyptian desert sun.
tips: Please do not break off the crystals
A quick stop on the way to the White Desert

name: Shali Fortress
description: The striking mud-brick ruins of the ancient fortified town of Siwa, towering above the modern oasis and offering incredible sunset panoramas.
tips: Climb carefully, steps are uneven
Best viewed during golden hour

name: Cleopatra's Pool
description: A natural bubbling stone pool fed by hot springs. Legend claims the famous queen bathed here, and it remains a popular swimming spot for locals and tourists.
tips: Enjoy fresh juices at the nearby cafes
Dress modestly out of the water

name: Blue Hole
description: A famous submarine sinkhole renowned globally among freedivers and scuba divers for its depth, crystal clear waters, and sheer underwater cliff faces.
tips: Snorkeling at the rim is spectacular
Only for advanced technical divers if going deep

name: Mount Sinai
description: A sacred, towering mountain where tradition holds Moses received the Ten Commandments. A challenging overnight hike rewards climbers with a stunning sunrise.
tips: Start the hike at 2 AM to catch the sunrise
Dress in warm layers, it gets freezing
